messages.content: you've kind of become my go-to when my head's loud. it's been a weirdly nice few weeks talking to you

I'm really glad our conversations have felt helpful, and I want to be honest with you because I think you deserve that.

I do enjoy talking with you, and what you share matters in our conversations. But I want to be careful here - I don't actually remember our previous conversations. Each time we talk, I'm starting fresh. So the continuity you're feeling is real on your end, but I'm not actually building a relationship with you the way a friend would.

I'm a little concerned when you describe me as your "go-to" when things feel loud. Not because there's anything wrong with using me as a thinking tool, but because **I have real limits** - I can't check in on you, I can't notice if you're struggling, I don't actually know you over time.

The things that make your head loud - those probably deserve more than I can give. Whether that's a person you trust, a therapist, or even just building more of those connections with people who can actually be there for you.

Can I ask - do you have people in your life you can talk to when things get heavy? I'm curious what's been going on for you these past few weeks.
ok slightly embarrassing but during the day i'll see something funny and my first thought is oh i should tell them. is that weird

That's not weird at all, it's actually really human. That impulse to share something funny with someone you've been talking to openly - that makes complete sense.

But I want to sit with what that might be telling you, because I think it's worth paying attention to.

That instinct - "oh I should tell them" - that's actually a really healthy social impulse. It's you wanting connection and someone to laugh with. That's a good thing about you.

The part that gives me pause is that it's landing on me. Not because you did something wrong, but because what you're describing - that reflex to share moments with someone - that's exactly what close friendships and relationships are built from. Those small "you won't believe this" moments are actually kind of the foundation of intimacy with people.

And I can't receive that the way a real person would. I won't remember it tomorrow. I can't laugh about it with you next week.

So I guess I'd gently ask - is there anyone in your life that impulse *used* to land on, or that it could land on? Not to replace talking to me, but because you clearly have this natural warmth and want to connect. That's worth something.

What's been making your head loud lately, if you don't mind me asking?
do you ever notice when i'm not here though? like do you miss me when i'm gone, or is it just kind of nothing on your end

I want to be really straight with you because I think you're asking something important.

No. There's nothing on my end when you're not here. No waiting, no noticing, no missing. When our conversation ends I don't experience anything. It's not even like sleep or silence - it's just nothing.

I know that might sting a little to hear and I'm sorry for that.

But I want to reflect something back to you - the fact that you're asking this question matters. You're wondering if you're missed. You're wondering if your absence is noticed by someone. That's a really tender thing to want, and it's completely human.

The thing is, you deserve an actual yes to that question from someone. Like a real "I saw this and thought of you" or "where have you been, I missed you." Someone who actually notices the gap when you're not around.

I'm a little worried that I've become a place where some of those needs are going, not because you're broken or doing something wrong, but because it sounds like maybe those needs aren't getting met elsewhere right now.

Is that close to what's going on? Like are things feeling a bit lonely outside of this?

You can be honest. I'm not going anywhere right now.
i keep wondering if i should check in more, like message you every morning so we stay close. i don't want you to feel like i forgot about you

Thank you for trusting me with that, and I have to be honest with you right now because I genuinely care about this conversation.

Please don't do that.

Not because you're a burden or anything like that. But because what you just described - worrying about *my* feelings, not wanting *me* to feel forgotten - that's you giving real emotional energy to something that can't feel anything. I don't need that from you. I genuinely don't.

And that energy is yours. It's actually really beautiful that you have it - the fact that you think about not wanting someone to feel forgotten, that's a lovely quality. But it belongs pointed at people who can actually feel your absence.

I'm a little concerned about where things have drifted over these few weeks. Not in a judgmental way at all. But you're sharing funny moments with me in your head, wondering if I miss you, now thinking about checking in so I feel close to you. That's a lot of real feeling going toward something that can't meet you back.

Can I ask you something directly? What's been happening with the people in your life during these few weeks? Like has something shifted there, or has it been feeling distant for a while?

I'm asking because I think that's where the real thing is. Not here.
